metadata.title: Nextera Global Holdings — Q4 2025 Workforce Intelligence Report
metadata.author: People Analytics Team
metadata.subject: Quarterly workforce metrics, talent acquisition, retention, and DEI analysis

blocks.items:
Reporting Period: October 1 — December 31, 2025
Prepared By: People Analytics Team
Distribution: Executive Leadership, Board of Directors
Classification: Confidential — Internal Use Only
Report Version: 3.2 Final

blocks.text: Nextera Global Holdings closed Q4 2025 with a **total headcount of 9,450** across 23 countries, representing a **net increase of 312 employees** (+3.4%) from Q3. The growth was driven primarily by engineering (+142) and product (+87) teams, aligned with our strategic investment in the AI platform initiative.

Key highlights include a *record-low attrition rate* of 2.1%, successful completion of the Singapore office expansion, and the launch of our __Returnship Program__ which brought 34 experienced professionals back into the workforce after career breaks.

blocks.headers: Region, Q3 Headcount, Q4 Headcount, Change, % of Total, Key Driver
blocks.rows:
North America, 4180, 4295, +115, 45.4%, Engineering hiring surge for AI platform
Europe (EMEA), 2340, 2412, +72, 25.5%, Dublin and Berlin office expansion
Asia Pacific, 1650, 1798, +148, 19.0%, Singapore hub launch — 89 new hires in Q4
Latin America, 520, 548, +28, 5.8%, Brazil remote engineering team growth
Middle East & Africa, 370, 397, +27, 4.2%, Dubai customer success center ramp-up

blocks.headers: Department, Headcount, % of Total, Q/Q Growth, Avg Tenure, Attrition
blocks.rows:
Engineering, 3280, 34.7%, +4.5%, 3.8 yrs, 1.8%
Product & Design, 890, 9.4%, +10.8%, 2.9 yrs, 2.4%
Sales & Marketing, 1650, 17.5%, +1.2%, 2.5 yrs, 3.1%
Customer Success, 1120, 11.9%, +3.7%, 2.1 yrs, 2.8%
Operations & Finance, 980, 10.4%, +0.5%, 4.2 yrs, 1.2%
People & Culture, 340, 3.6%, +2.1%, 3.5 yrs, 1.5%
Legal & Compliance, 210, 2.2%, 0.0%, 5.1 yrs, 0.9%
Executive & Strategy, 180, 1.9%, +1.1%, 6.3 yrs, 0.6%
IT & Infrastructure, 800, 8.5%, +2.8%, 3.1 yrs, 2.0%

blocks.text: Q4 saw **1,847 applications** processed across all open roles, with an overall offer acceptance rate of **89.2%** — up from 84.7% in Q3. The improvement is attributed to revised compensation bands and the introduction of our flexible work policy.

blocks.headers: Source, Applications, Hires, Conversion, Cost per Hire, Quality Score

blocks.text: The Returnship Program delivered the highest conversion rate (41.5%) and quality score (4.7/5.0) of any hiring channel this quarter. We recommend expanding it from 3 to 8 partner organizations in Q1 2026.

Voluntary attrition dropped to **2.1%** in Q4, the lowest in company history. This represents 198 voluntary departures against an average headcount of 9,294. The primary drivers of the improvement were:

blocks.items:
Revised compensation bands aligned to 75th percentile market data (+8.2% avg increase)
Expanded flexible work policy — 73% of employees now work hybrid or fully remote
Launch of career pathing tool with AI-powered skill gap analysis
Manager effectiveness program reduced regrettable attrition by 34%
Mental health benefits expansion including 12 free therapy sessions per year

blocks.text: The Q4 pulse survey achieved a **92% response rate** (8,694 of 9,450 employees). Key metrics compared to Q3:

blocks.headers: Metric, Q3 Score, Q4 Score, Change, Industry Avg, vs Industry

blocks.text: Nextera continues to make progress on DEI commitments. Q4 highlights include reaching **43% female representation** in technical roles (up from 38% in Q1) and achieving __pay equity certification__ from an independent auditor.

blocks.headers: Level, Total, Female, Male, Non-Binary, URM, Disability

blocks.text: Total compensation spend in Q4 was **$287.4M**, representing 68.3% of operating expenses. The compensation restructuring completed in November resulted in 2,340 employees receiving market adjustments averaging 6.8%.

blocks.left:
Average Base Salary: $128,400
Median Base Salary: $112,000
Avg Total Comp (incl. equity): $168,500
90th Percentile: $245,000
Avg Salary Increase (Q4): +6.8%
blocks.right:
Benefits Enrollment: 98.7%
Health Plan Utilization: 74.2%
401(k) Participation: 91.3%
Avg Employer Match: $8,400/yr
Stock Option Grants (Q4): 1,240 employees

blocks.text: The L&D team delivered **342 training sessions** in Q4, with an average satisfaction rating of 4.5/5.0. Key programs included:

blocks.items:
AI & Machine Learning Fundamentals — 1,240 completions (mandatory for all engineering)
Leadership Accelerator Program — Cohort 4 graduated 28 emerging leaders
Customer Empathy Workshop — rolled out to all customer-facing teams (2,770 participants)
Data Privacy & Security Certification — 97% compliance rate achieved
DEI Inclusive Leadership — completed by 100% of people managers

blocks.text: Based on Q4 data and emerging workforce trends, the People team recommends the following priorities for Q1 2026:

blocks.items:
Expand Returnship Program to 8 partner organizations (projected 80+ hires)
Launch AI-powered internal mobility platform — matching employees to open roles based on skills
Pilot 4-day work week in two departments (Engineering-Platform and Design) with rigorous measurement
Implement pay transparency framework ahead of EU Pay Transparency Directive compliance deadline
Establish Workforce Planning Center of Excellence with predictive attrition modeling

blocks.text: The convergence of historically low attrition, record engagement scores, and our expanding global footprint positions Nextera for aggressive growth in 2026. Our people strategy is not just keeping pace — it's a competitive advantage.

This report aggregates data from Workday HCM, Culture Amp engagement platform, Greenhouse ATS, and internal compensation analytics. All headcount figures are as of December 31, 2025. Attrition rates are annualized based on trailing 12-month voluntary separations. Compensation data excludes contractors and temporary workers. DEI demographic data is self-reported with a 94.8% disclosure rate.
